Data analysis: Meta-analysis of the influence of ENSO phases on yield-loss caused by soybean rust in Brazil

Author
Affiliation
Kaique S. Alves

Universidade Federal de Viçosa, Viçosa MG, Brazil

Published

August 2, 2022

Packages

library(tidyverse)
library(gsheet)
library(cowplot)
library(patchwork)
library(lemon)
library(lme4)
library(ggforce)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)
library(ggthemes)
library(metafor)
library(minpack.lm)
theme_set(theme_half_open())

Data

Soybean Rust data

data_load = gsheet2tbl("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1eGcLU8JVJ485pkKgAQUuVorapp57TVvYtRO5hpnmW3w/edit#gid=1365448637")
Warning: One or more parsing issues, see `problems()` for details

ENSO data

enso_data = gsheet2tbl("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1NFm1hKCS8V4r3GRbE13QbKAQ8RZ0e21WES6UtN7uM_8/edit?usp=sharing")

Classifying the years based on Oceanic Niño Index (ONI) on the October, November, and December (OND) trimester. Seasons with ONI higher then 75 percentiles were classified as warm, year with ONI lower then its 25 percentiles were classified as Cold, and the years with ONI within the 25 and 75 percentiles were classified as neutral.

enso_data_class = enso_data %>%
  mutate(year = as.character(Year+1)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-Year) %>% 
  dplyr::select(OND, year) %>%
  filter(!is.na(OND),
         year != 2021) %>% 
  mutate(enso = case_when(OND > quantile(OND, 0.75) ~ "Warm",
                          OND < quantile(OND, 0.25) ~ "Cold",
                          OND < quantile(OND, 0.75) & OND > quantile(OND, 0.25) ~ "Neutral"),
         year = as.numeric(year))
enso_data_class
# A tibble: 16 × 3
     OND  year enso   
   <dbl> <dbl> <chr>  
 1   0.7  2005 Neutral
 2  -0.6  2006 Neutral
 3   0.9  2007 Warm   
 4  -1.5  2008 Cold   
 5  -0.6  2009 Neutral
 6   1.4  2010 Warm   
 7  -1.6  2011 Cold   
 8  -1.1  2012 Cold   
 9   0.1  2013 Neutral
10  -0.2  2014 Neutral
11   0.6  2015 Neutral
12   2.6  2016 Warm   
13  -0.7  2017 Neutral
14  -0.8  2018 Cold   
15   0.9  2019 Warm   
16   0.5  2020 Neutral
enso_data %>%
  mutate(year = as.character(Year+1)) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-Year) %>% 
  dplyr::select(OND, year) %>%
  filter(!is.na(OND),
         year != 2021) %>% 
  summarise(low = quantile(OND, 0.25),
            high =  quantile(OND, 0.75) )
# A tibble: 1 × 2
     low  high
   <dbl> <dbl>
1 -0.725  0.75
enso_data_class%>% 
  mutate(enso = factor(enso, levels = c("Neutral", "Warm", "Cold")))%>% 
  ggplot(aes( as.factor(year),OND,  color = enso))+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0)+
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(-0.725,0.75), linetype = 2, color = "gray")+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=0, ymax  = OND), width = 0, color = "black")+
  geom_point(size = 3)+
  
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  theme_half_open(font_size = 12)+
  # background_grid()+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(-0.725,0.75), limits = c(-2,2.6))+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        # legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_blank())+
  labs(x = "Crop Season",
       y = "Oceanic Niño Index",
       color = "ENSO phase (OND)")

ggsave("figs/ONI_OND.png", dpi = 600, height = 4, width = 7, bg = "white")

Counting the number of year for each phase

enso_data_class %>% 
  count(enso)
# A tibble: 3 × 2
  enso        n
  <chr>   <int>
1 Cold        4
2 Neutral     8
3 Warm        4

Data wrangling

data_load2 = data_load %>%
  # mutate(enso = case_when(year %in% c(2007,2010,2016,2019) ~ "EL Nino",
  #                         year %in% c(2008,2011, 2012,2021) ~ "La Nina",
  #                         !year %in% c(2007,2010,2016,2019,2008,2011, 2012,2021)~"Neutral"),
  #        enso = factor(enso, levels = c("Neutral", "EL Nino", "La Nina"))
  # ) %>% 
  full_join(enso_data_class) %>% 
  mutate(enso = factor(enso, levels = c("Neutral", "Warm", "Cold"))) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(sev),
         !is.na(yld)) %>% 
  mutate(study = as.factor(study)) %>% 
  mutate(region = case_when(state %in% c("SP","BA","MG", "MS", "MT", "GO",  "MA", "DF", "TO")~"North",
                             state %in% c("RS","SC","PR") ~"South"),
         region =factor(region, levels = c("South","North"))) %>% 
  group_by(study) %>% 
  mutate(difer = max(sev) - min(sev)) %>% 
  filter(difer>5)
Joining, by = "year"
data_load2 %>% 
  filter(active_ingred == "CHECK") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(sev, difer))+
  geom_point()

unique(data_load2$state)
 [1] "BA" "PR" "MG" "MS" "MT" "GO" "SP" "MA" "DF" "TO" "RS" "SC"
length(unique(data_load2$state))
[1] 12
data_load2 %>% 
  group_by(state) %>% 
  summarise(n_loc = length(unique(location))) %>% 
  summarise(sum(n_loc))
# A tibble: 1 × 1
  `sum(n_loc)`
         <int>
1           73
length(unique(data_load2$year))
[1] 16

Tranformations

Converting percent severity into proportion and calculating logits

only_check_df = data_load2 %>% 
  filter(active_ingred == "CHECK") %>% 
  mutate(sev = case_when(sev == 100 ~ 99.9,
                         sev == 0 ~ 0.1,
                         sev>0 & sev<100 ~ sev), 
           logit_sev =  DescTools::Logit(sev/100))

Modeling Severity (untreated) and ENSO

enso_sev_gg= data_load2 %>% 
  filter(active_ingred == "CHECK") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(enso, sev, color = enso))+
  geom_sina(color = "gray")+
  geom_boxplot(fill= NA, size = 1)+
  theme_half_open(font_size = 12)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  labs( y = "",
        x = "",
        color = "",
        title = "")
enso_sev_gg

Graph over time

sev_untreat_gg = only_check_df %>% 
  ggplot(aes(as.factor(year),  sev, color = enso))+
  geom_sina(color = "gray80")+
  geom_boxplot(fill =NA, size = 1, outlier.color = NA)+
  labs( y = "Severity (%)",
        x = "",
        color = "",
        title = " ")+
  theme_half_open(font_size = 12)+
  # facet_wrap(~region)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  theme(
    # axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1)
    )
sev_untreat_gg

# ggsave("figs/severity_check.png", dpi = 600, height = 4, width = 10, bg = "white")

Mixed-effect model

model_check = lmer(logit_sev ~ enso+ (1|year/study), data = only_check_df,REML = F)

Model summary

summary(model_check)
Linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood . t-tests use Satterthwaite's
  method [lmerModLmerTest]
Formula: logit_sev ~ enso + (1 | year/study)
   Data: only_check_df

     AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
  4506.7   4539.3  -2247.3   4494.7     1680 

Scaled residuals: 
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-7.5458 -0.2094  0.0000  0.2012  9.5043 

Random effects:
 Groups     Name        Variance Std.Dev.
 study:year (Intercept) 2.80243  1.6740  
 year       (Intercept) 0.07201  0.2683  
 Residual               0.35208  0.5934  
Number of obs: 1686, groups:  study:year, 418; year, 16

Fixed effects:
            Estimate Std. Error      df t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)   0.6678     0.1523 13.0217   4.385 0.000735 ***
ensoWarm      0.2028     0.2544 11.8761   0.797 0.441117    
ensoCold      0.4745     0.2843 16.4863   1.669 0.113967    
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Correlation of Fixed Effects:
         (Intr) ensWrm
ensoWarm -0.599       
ensoCold -0.536  0.321

Pairwise comparison

pairs(emmeans(model_check, ~enso))
 contrast       estimate    SE   df t.ratio p.value
 Neutral - Warm   -0.203 0.290 18.6  -0.699  0.7671
 Neutral - Cold   -0.474 0.320 23.0  -1.485  0.3165
 Warm - Cold      -0.272 0.355 21.4  -0.764  0.7283

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates

Mixed-effect model

model_check_yld = lmer(yld ~ enso + (1|year/study), data = only_check_df, REML = F)

Model summary

summary(model_check_yld)
Linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood . t-tests use Satterthwaite's
  method [lmerModLmerTest]
Formula: yld ~ enso + (1 | year/study)
   Data: only_check_df

     AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
 24878.7  24911.3 -12433.4  24866.7     1680

    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-4.5778 -0.4421  0.0067  0.4548  5.2378 

Random effects:
 Groups     Name        Variance Std.Dev.
 study:year (Intercept) 618988   786.8   
 year       (Intercept)  96443   310.6   
 Residual                57242   239.3   
Number of obs: 1686, groups:  study:year, 418; year, 16

Fixed effects:
            Estimate Std. Error      df t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  2148.18     123.74   14.70  17.361 3.36e-11 ***
ensoWarm     -219.25     211.23   13.94  -1.038    0.317    
ensoCold      181.70     221.18   16.40   0.822    0.423    
---

ensoCold -0.559  0.328

Pairwise comparison

cld(emmeans(model_check_yld, ~enso))
 enso    emmean  SE   df lower.CL upper.CL .group
 Warm      1929 192 18.9     1527     2331  1    
 Neutral   2148 138 19.8     1860     2437  1    
 Cold      2330 203 22.0     1908     2752  1    

Degrees-of-freedom method: kenward-roger 
Confidence level used: 0.95 
P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates 
significance level used: alpha = 0.05 
NOTE: Compact letter displays can be misleading
      because they show NON-findings rather than findings.
      Consider using 'pairs()', 'pwpp()', or 'pwpm()' instead.

Data combo

sev_untreat_gg+ enso_sev_gg+
  yld_untreat_gg +enso_yld_gg+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")+
  plot_layout(ncol = 2,
              widths = c(1,0.25),
              guides = "collect")&
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size =8))

ggsave("figs/data_over_time.png", dpi = 600, height = 6, width = 9, bg = "white")

Modeling disease damage

Yield vs. Severity

data_load2 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(sev, yld, color = enso))+
  # geom_point(alpha = 0.1)+
  geom_smooth(aes(group=study),
              method = "lm", size =0.1,se = F, fullrange   =T)+
  geom_smooth(aes(group = enso),  color = "red", se = F, method = "lm")+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  theme_half_open()+
  facet_rep_grid(~enso)+
  ylim(0,7500)+
  labs(x = "Severity (%)",
       y = "Yield (kg/ha)",
       color = "")+
  theme(strip.background = element_blank())
`geom_smooth()` using formula 'y ~ x'
`geom_smooth()` using formula 'y ~ x'
Warning: Removed 1124 rows containing missing values (geom_smooth).

Meta-analysis

Ordinary Regression

reg_dc = data_load2 %>% 
  group_by(study, year, region, enso) %>% 
  summarise(intercept = lm(yld~sev)$coefficients[1],
            slope = lm(yld~sev)$coefficients[2],
            r2 = summary(lm(yld~sev))$r.squared,
            sigma = summary(lm(yld~sev))$sigma) %>%
  mutate(Dc = (slope/intercept)*100) %>% 
  filter(Dc<0.5)
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'study', 'year', 'region'. You can override
using the `.groups` argument.
reg_dc
# A tibble: 417 × 9
# Groups:   study, year, region [417]
   study  year region enso    intercept   slope         r2 sigma      Dc
   <fct> <dbl> <fct>  <fct>       <dbl>   <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>
 1 1      2005 North  Neutral     3197. -27.4   0.565       377. -0.858 
 2 2      2005 South  Neutral     2132.  -5.89  0.0299      323. -0.276 
 3 3      2005 North  Neutral     2551. -12.8   0.309       277. -0.503 
 4 4      2005 North  Neutral     1322.   0.311 0.00000713  156.  0.0236
 5 5      2005 North  Neutral     2483. -23.0   0.607       334. -0.928 
 6 6      2005 North  Neutral     2640.  -6.47  0.0162      406. -0.245 
 7 7      2005 North  Neutral     2539. -29.1   0.0392      490. -1.15  
 8 8      2005 South  Neutral     2475. -67.9   0.147       342. -2.74  
 9 9      2005 North  Neutral     2521. -30.5   0.759       298. -1.21  
10 10     2005 North  Neutral     2171.  -9.26  0.0338      230. -0.427 
# … with 407 more rows

Graph of the regression lines

reg1_gg = reg_dc %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_abline(aes(intercept = intercept, slope = slope, color= enso), alpha = 0.9)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  labs(x = "Severity (%)",
       y = "Yield (kg/ha)")+
  theme_half_open()+
  xlim(0,100)+
  ylim(0,7500)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  theme_half_open(font_size = 10)+
  facet_rep_grid(~enso)+
  labs(x = "SBR severity (%)",
       y = "Soybean yield (kg/ha)",
       color = "")+
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none")
Scale for 'colour' is already present. Adding another scale for 'colour',
which will replace the existing scale.
reg1_gg

Distribution of intercepts and slopes

reg_dc %>% 
  ggplot(aes(intercept))+
reg_dc %>% 
  ggplot(aes(slope))+
   
#patchwork
plot_layout(nrow = 1)+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")&
  
#more ggplot  
  geom_histogram(color = "white", bins = 10)&
  theme_half_open()

Percent relative yield loss relative to β0

data2 = data_load2 %>% 
  full_join(reg_dc) %>% 
  mutate(l = 100*((intercept - yld)/intercept)) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(l))
Joining, by = c("study", "year", "enso", "region")
data2
# A tibble: 26,541 × 21
# Groups:   study [417]
   study  year location state   rep n_spray brand_name group active_ingred   sev
   <fct> <dbl> <chr>    <chr> <dbl>   <dbl> <chr>      <chr> <chr>         <dbl>
 1 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        1      NA Alert      DMI_… flusilazole …     2
 2 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        2      NA Alert      DMI_… flusilazole …     9
 3 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        3      NA Alert      DMI_… flusilazole …    14
 4 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        4      NA Alert      DMI_… flusilazole …     1
 5 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        1      NA Artea      DMI_… cyproconazol…     7
 6 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        2      NA Artea      DMI_… cyproconazol…     1
 7 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        3      NA Artea      DMI_… cyproconazol…     1
 8 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        4      NA Artea      DMI_… cyproconazol…     1
 9 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        1      NA Celeiro    DMI_… flutriafol +…     7
10 1      2005 LUÍS ED… BA        2      NA Celeiro    DMI_… flutriafol +…     1
# … with 26,531 more rows, and 11 more variables: yld <dbl>, OND <dbl>,
#   enso <fct>, region <fct>, difer <dbl>, intercept <dbl>, slope <dbl>,
#   r2 <dbl>, sigma <dbl>, Dc <dbl>, l <dbl>
data2 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(sev,l))+
  geom_point(color = "gray90")+
  geom_smooth(method ="lm", aes(color =enso), formula = y~0+x)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  labs(x = "Severity (%)",
       y = expression("Yield loss relative to "~β[0]))+
  theme_half_open()

Second Regression l ~ 0 + sev

data3 = data2 %>% 
  group_by(study, year, enso, region) %>% 
  summarise(slope = lm(l~0+sev)$coefficients,
            vi = as.numeric(vcov(lm(l~0+sev)))
            ) %>% 
  full_join(enso_data_class)
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'study', 'year', 'enso'. You can override
using the `.groups` argument.
Joining, by = c("year", "enso")
data3
# A tibble: 417 × 7
# Groups:   study, year, enso [417]
   study  year enso    region   slope      vi   OND
   <fct> <dbl> <chr>   <fct>    <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>
 1 1      2005 Neutral North   0.858  0.00741   0.7
 2 2      2005 Neutral South   0.276  0.0336    0.7
 3 3      2005 Neutral North   0.503  0.00854   0.7
 4 4      2005 Neutral North  -0.0236 1.48      0.7
 5 5      2005 Neutral North   0.928  0.00711   0.7
 6 6      2005 Neutral North   0.245  0.0229    0.7
 7 7      2005 Neutral North   1.15   0.557     0.7
 8 8      2005 Neutral South   2.74   0.721     0.7
 9 9      2005 Neutral North   1.21   0.00546   0.7
10 10     2005 Neutral North   0.427  0.0202    0.7
# … with 407 more rows
reg2_gg =
  data3 %>% 
  ggplot()+
  geom_abline(aes(intercept = 0, slope = slope, color= enso), alpha = 0.9)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  labs(x = "Severity (%)",
       y = "Yield (kg/ha)")+
  theme_half_open()+
  xlim(0,100)+
  ylim(0,100)+
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0),
                     limits = c(0,100)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0),
                     limits = c(0,100))+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  theme_half_open(font_size = 10)+
  facet_rep_grid(~enso)+
  labs(x = "SBR severity (%)",
       y = expression("Yield loss relative to "~β[0]~"(%)"),
       color = "")+
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none")

Damage vs. ENSO phases

data3 %>% 
  ggplot(aes(slope))+
  geom_histogram(color = "gray")+
  facet_rep_wrap(~enso, ncol=1 )
`stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.

library(scales)

Attaching package: 'scales'
The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':

    discard
The following object is masked from 'package:readr':

    col_factor
slopes_gg = data3 %>% 
  arrange(slope) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(enso, slope ))+
  geom_boxplot(fill = NA, color = "steelblue", size =1, outlier.colour = NA)+
  geom_sina(aes(size = vi, color= vi), alpha = 0.5)+
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 7),
                        #limits =  c(0.01,1),
                        breaks = c(0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1),
                       label=scientific_format())+
  scale_color_gradient(low = "black",
                       high = "red",
                       #limits = c(0.01, 1), 
                        breaks = c(0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1),
                       label=scientific_format())+
  guides(color= guide_legend(), 
         size=guide_legend())+
  theme_half_open(font_size = 10)+
  labs(y = "Slope",
       x = "",
       size = "Slope variance",
       color  = "Slope variance" )
slopes_gg

(reg1_gg+reg2_gg)+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")+
  plot_layout(ncol = 1)

ggsave("figs/regression_lines1.png", dpi = 900, width = 8, height = 5, bg ="white")
layout <- "
AAAACC
BBBBCC
"

(reg1_gg+reg2_gg+slopes_gg)+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")+
  plot_layout(design = layout)

ggsave("figs/regression_lines.png", dpi = 600, width = 8, height = 5, bg ="white")

Meta-analytical model

data4 = data3 %>% mutate(year = as.factor(year))
metamodel2 = rma.uni(yi = slope,
                    vi = vi,
                    mods = ~ 0+enso, 
                    random = list(~1|year/study),
                    # struct = "HCS",
                    method = "ML",
                    data =data4)
Warning: Extra argument ('random') disregarded.
metamodel2

Mixed-Effects Model (k = 417; tau^2 estimator: ML)

tau^2 (estimated amount of residual heterogeneity):     0.1099 (SE = 0.0080)
tau (square root of estimated tau^2 value):             0.3316
I^2 (residual heterogeneity / unaccounted variability): 99.54%
H^2 (unaccounted variability / sampling variability):   219.29

Test for Residual Heterogeneity:
QE(df = 414) = 41096.3735, p-val < .0001

Test of Moderators (coefficients 1:3):
QM(df = 3) = 1258.6134, p-val < .0001

Model Results:

             estimate      se     zval    pval   ci.lb   ci.ub     ​ 
ensoCold       0.5011  0.0382  13.1116  <.0001  0.4262  0.5760  *** 
ensoNeutral    0.5651  0.0236  23.9141  <.0001  0.5188  0.6114  *** 
ensoWarm       0.6909  0.0305  22.6896  <.0001  0.6312  0.7506  *** 

---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Estimates for each moderator

grid = qdrg(object = metamodel2,  data = data4, at = list(vi = 0, year.c = 0))
# cld(emmeans(grid,specs = ~enso, by = "region"), Letters = letters)
cld(emmeans(grid, specs = ~enso), Letters = letters)
 enso    emmean     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL .group
 Cold     0.501 0.0382 Inf     0.426     0.576  a    
 Neutral  0.565 0.0236 Inf     0.519     0.611  a    
 Warm     0.691 0.0305 Inf     0.631     0.751   b   

Confidence level used: 0.95 
P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates 
significance level used: alpha = 0.05 
NOTE: Compact letter displays can be misleading
      because they show NON-findings rather than findings.
      Consider using 'pairs()', 'pwpp()', or 'pwpm()' instead. 
# grid@V

Graphs

Forest plot

forest_gg = data3 %>% 
  mutate(study = as.character(study),
         sd = sqrt(vi),
         
         cil = slope - 1.96*sd,
         ciu = slope + 1.96*sd) %>% 
  full_join(as.data.frame(emmeans(grid,specs = ~enso))) %>% 
  mutate(enso = factor(enso, levels = c("Neutral",  "Warm","Cold"))) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(slope,reorder(study, slope),  color= enso))+
  geom_point(size=0.3)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=cil, xmax = ciu), width = 0,size=0.3, alpha =0.5)+
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed")+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = emmean), color = "gray40", size = .5)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  facet_rep_grid(rows = vars(enso),  scales="free_y")+
  labs(y = "",
       x = "Damage coefficient (%/p.p.)",
       color = "")+
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size=2.5)))+
  theme_half_open()+
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0, "cm"),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right")
Joining, by = "enso"
forest_gg

# ggsave("figs/z_studies.png", dpi = 600, width = 5, height = 8, bg ="white")

Damage coefficient

dc_data =  as.data.frame(emmeans(grid, specs = ~enso))
dc_data
     enso    emmean         SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
1    Cold 0.5011073 0.03821859 Inf 0.4262003 0.5760144
2 Neutral 0.5650787 0.02362955 Inf 0.5187656 0.6113917
3    Warm 0.6909191 0.03045097 Inf 0.6312363 0.7506019
DC_gg = as.data.frame(emmeans(grid,specs = ~enso)) %>% 
    mutate(enso = factor(enso, levels = c("Neutral",  "Warm","Cold"))) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(reorder(enso,emmean), emmean, color = enso))+
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.2), size= 3)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin =asymp.LCL, ymax = asymp.UCL),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.2),
                width = 0,
                size = 1)+
  labs(x = "ENSO phases",
       y = "Damage coefficient (%/p.p.)",
       color = "")+
  theme_half_open()+
  # background_grid()+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0.1,0.8, by = 0.1), limits =c(0.4,0.8))+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  theme(legend.position = "none")
  
 
DC_gg

Yield loss

rel_gg = as.data.frame(emmeans(grid,specs = ~enso)) %>% 
    mutate(enso = factor(enso, levels = c("Neutral",  "Warm","Cold"))) %>% 
  mutate(`100` = 100-100*emmean,
         `0` = 100) %>% 
  pivot_longer(7:8,
               names_to = "sev", 
               values_to = "yloss") %>%
  mutate(sev = as.numeric(sev)) %>%
  mutate(cil = 100-sev*asymp.LCL,
         ciu = 100-sev*asymp.UCL) %>% 
  mutate(yl = -(yloss-100)) %>% 
  # mutate(cil = ifelse(cil==100, cil, -cil),
  #        ciu = ifelse(ciu==100, ciu, -ciu)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(sev, yloss,color =  enso, fill = enso ))+
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = cil, ymax = ciu),alpha = 0.5, color = NA)+
  geom_line(size = 1)+
  # geom_line(aes(sev, yl, linetype = "Yield loss"))+
  scale_linetype_manual(values = 2)+
  scale_color_colorblind()+
  scale_fill_colorblind()+
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,100))+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,100),
                     # sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . * 1, name = "Yield loss (%)")
                     )+
  # facet_rep_wrap(~enso)+
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none")+
  coord_equal()+
  labs(x = "Severity (%)",
       y = "Soybean yield (%)",
       color = "ENSO phase",
       fill = "ENSO phase")
rel_gg

Combo plot

 ((DC_gg/rel_gg) | forest_gg )+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")+
  # plot_layout(guides = "collect")&
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("figs/z.png", dpi = 900, width =7, height = 7.5, bg ="white")

Yield Gain

Vector for attainable yield (ymax), control efficacy (lambda), severity in untreated field (sn), and damage coefficient (a).

ymax = seq(1500, 4000,length.out = 200)
lambda = c(30, 50, 70)
sn = seq(0, 1,length.out = 200)
a = dc_data$emmean

Calculating yield gain (yld_gain) for the grid of vectors

ygain_df = expand.grid(ymax = ymax,lambda = lambda, sn = sn*100, a = a) %>% 
  mutate(yld_gain = (a*ymax*(sn - sn*((1-lambda/100))))/100) %>% 
  mutate(lambda = paste0(lambda,"% of Control")) %>% 
  full_join(dc_data %>% rename(a = emmean))
Joining, by = "a"

Response surfaces

Absolute yield gain

ygain_df %>% 
  ggplot(aes(sn, ymax, fill = yld_gain))+ 
  geom_raster()+
  # scale_fill_distiller(palette = "Spectral")+
  # scale_fill_fermenter(palette = "Spectral")+
  scale_fill_viridis_b(option = "A", 
                       guide = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 15, barheight = 0.3),
                       breaks = seq(0, 3000, by =250)
                       )+
  facet_grid(lambda~enso)+
  theme_minimal_grid(font_size = 10)+
  labs(y = "Attainable yield (kg/ha)",
       x = "Severity untreated (%)",
       fill ="Yield gain (kg/ha)" )+
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave("figs/surface_yield_gain.png", height =6, width = 6, bg = "white" )

Yield gain difference from Neutral ENSO

ygain_df %>% 
  group_by(enso) %>% 
  mutate(id = 1:length(enso)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(id,lambda, sn,ymax),
              values_from = yld_gain,
              names_from = enso) %>% 
  mutate(Cold = Cold - Neutral,
         Warm = Warm -Neutral) %>% 
  dplyr::select(-Neutral) %>% 
  pivot_longer(5:6,
               values_to = "diff",
               names_to = "enso") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(sn, ymax, fill = diff))+ 
  geom_raster()+
  scale_fill_steps2(midpoint = 0,
                    low = "#7b3014",
                    mid = "white",
                    high = "#27456e",
                    guide = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 13, barheight = 0.3),
                       breaks = seq(-300, 400, by =50)
                       )+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(min(ymax), max(ymax), length.out = 5))+
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(sn)*100, max(sn)*100, length.out = 5 ))+
  facet_grid(lambda~enso)+
  theme_minimal_grid(font_size = 10)+
  labs(y = "Attainable yield (kg/ha)",
       x = "Severity untreated (%)",
       fill ="Yield gain difference from\nNeutral ENSO phase (kg/ha)" )+
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.text = element_text(size =5),
        legend.text = element_text(size =5),
        legend.title = element_text(size =5))

ggsave("figs/surface_yield_gain_difference.png", height =5, width = 4, bg = "white" )
